stem: A certain apprentice has enrolled in 96 hours of training courses. The equation 12x + 20y = 96 represents this situation, where x is the number of on-site training courses and y is the number of online training courses this apprentice has enrolled in. How many more hours does each online training course take than each on-site training course?
rationale: The coefficient of x in the equation is the number of hours per on-site course, and the coefficient of y is the number of hours per online course. So each on-site course takes 12 hours and each online course takes 20 hours, a difference of 20 − 12 = 8 hours.
